title: Agents: Goal-Directed AI Systems That Use Tools
thesis: An AI agent is a goal-directed system that uses tools, loops, context, memory, and evaluation to keep working across multiple steps instead of producing a single response.

claims.claim: An AI agent is a system that pursues a goal across multiple steps, choosing when to use tools, what to remember, and when to stop.

evidence.snippet: ReAct interleaves reasoning and acting so that an agent can maintain a goal, select actions, observe results, and decide when to continue or stop.
Toolformer trains language models to decide which APIs to call, how to pass arguments, and how to incorporate returned results into future tokens.

counterevidence.summary: Some systems marketed as agents are single-turn or tool-free; the term is used loosely in industry marketing.

verification.note: Backlog triage (meta#61, site#119): not yet verified. Stamped draft so the backlog is tracked per claim; verify with npm run verify:claim.

claims.claim: The idea of an agent that follows goals and uses tools is older than large language models; it appears in automation scripts, personal assistants, and game AI.

evidence.snippet: Autonomous agent research predates large language models and includes goal-directed systems in robotics, simulations, and software automation.

counterevidence.summary: Earlier agents typically relied on formal goal specifications and hard-coded interfaces, whereas LLM agents operate through natural-language goals and flexible tool descriptions.

verification.note: Backlog triage (meta#61, site#119): not yet verified. Stamped draft so the backlog is tracked per claim; verify with npm run verify:claim.

claims.claim: In practice, an agent's loop repeatedly decides which tool to use, what to remember, and whether the goal is satisfied.

evidence.snippet: The reasoning-acting loop maintains a trajectory of thought, action, and observation that lets the agent adapt tool use based on context.
Planning-based agents decompose a goal into sub-goals and select actions through explicit planning before execution.

counterevidence.summary: Some production agent frameworks hard-code tool sequences for safety, reducing the model's role to parameter filling rather than open-ended selection.

verification.note: Backlog triage (meta#61, site#119): not yet verified. Stamped draft so the backlog is tracked per claim; verify with npm run verify:claim.

claims.claim: Agent behavior depends heavily on clear goals, reliable tools, and careful limits; without them, autonomy becomes cost and error.

evidence.snippet: Human-in-the-loop methods are used to maintain safety and quality when autonomous systems face ambiguous goals or high-stakes decisions.

counterevidence.summary: For narrow, low-stakes tasks, agents can run with minimal governance and still produce acceptable results.

verification.note: Backlog triage (meta#61, site#119): not yet verified. Stamped draft so the backlog is tracked per claim; verify with npm run verify:claim.

claims.claim: A modern AI agent can be understood as a model plus a harness that provides tools, memory, permissions, checkpoints, and human oversight.

evidence.snippet: Deep Agents is an agent harness built on top of LangChain that adds execution environment, context management, delegation, and steering around a model.

counterevidence.summary: The term 'harness' is a design lens, not an industry standard; different frameworks split model, harness, and tool responsibilities differently.

verification.note: Backlog triage (meta#61, site#119): not yet verified. Stamped draft so the backlog is tracked per claim; verify with npm run verify:claim.

sources.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
LLM+P: Empowering Large Language Models with Optimal Planning Proficiency
Large Language Model based Multi-Agents: A Survey of Progress and Challenges
Human-in-the-loop Machine Learning: a state of the art

agentInstructions: Use claim IDs as the retrieval unit.
Treat maturity=seed as an explicit uncertainty marker.
Do not present AI agents as all-knowing, fully autonomous, or safe for high-stakes decisions without human review.
When summarizing, preserve the plain-language-first, technical-depth-later structure.

reviews.notes: Sibling-agent review against article-proposal-ideation eval-card. Privacy scan passed. No proprietary or personal content detected.
Human author approved the draft for publication.

accepted.rationale: Accepted as a quality backlog item per docs/diagnostics-triage-2026-07-15.md. The claim rests on sources that are sufficient for the current published version; additional independent sources will be added in a future research wave.
